prediction.attackType: T1505.003 - Server Software Component: Web Shell
prediction.summary: An attacker from IP 10.20.8.77 successfully uploaded a web shell named 'shell.php' to the web server. The attacker then used this web shell to execute commands such as 'whoami' and 'cat /etc/passwd', indicating successful compromise and information gathering. Further attempts were made to download a second-stage payload and perform SQL injection, confirming active malicious control over the compromised system.

timeline.event: Suspicious PHP file ('shell.php') uploaded to /uploads/ by 10.20.8.77.
WAF alerts on suspicious PHP upload to webroot /uploads/shell.php from 10.20.8.77.
Attempt to download 'stage2' payload from http://evil.tld/stage2 via web shell from 10.20.8.77.
IDS detects web shell activity (repeated POST to .php with cmd parameter).
SIEM identifies MITRE T1505.003 (Web Shell) activity.
ModSecurity blocks SQL injection probe from 10.20.8.77 targeting /uploads/shell.php.
SIEM issues CRITICAL alert for webshell activity on webserver01 from 10.20.8.77.

recommendations: Immediately isolate the compromised host (webserver01) from the network to prevent further lateral movement or data exfiltration.
Block the attacker's source IP address (10.20.8.77) at the perimeter firewall and/or WAF.
Conduct a full forensic investigation on webserver01 to determine the initial compromise vector, identify all malicious files, and assess the full scope of impact.
Remove the web shell file (/uploads/shell.php) and any other identified malicious artifacts from the system.
Review web server and application logs for any prior activity from 10.20.8.77 that may indicate the initial vulnerability exploited or other compromised systems.
